chunk_summary: This section discusses bone health monitoring in children on dairy-poor vegetarian diets, focusing on vitamin D and calcium deficiencies, and referencing guidelines for prevention and treatment of rickets and related conditions.
learning_objectives: Understand the impact of dairy-poor vegetarian diets on bone health in children.
Learn the recommended guidelines for preventing and treating vitamin D and calcium deficiencies.
Identify key diagnostic and monitoring strategies for bone health in children with limited dairy intake.
key_facts: Vitamin D deficiency is a leading cause of rickets in children, especially in regions with limited dairy consumption.
The Indian Academy of Pediatrics (IAP) recommends 400-600 IU/day of vitamin D supplementation for children at risk.
Calcium intake should meet the recommended dietary allowance (RDA) of 500-1300 mg/day depending on age.
Plant-based diets may lack bioavailable calcium and vitamin D, necessitating fortified foods or supplements.
Regular monitoring of serum 25(OH)D levels is essential for children on dairy-poor diets to prevent bone health complications.
reference_quote: The Indian Academy of Pediatrics (IAP) revised guideline 2021 recommends 400-600 IU/day of vitamin D supplementation for children to prevent rickets and related conditions.
document_key_topics: Vitamin D and calcium deficiencies in children
Bone health monitoring in dairy-poor diets
Guidelines for prevention and treatment of rickets
